creator: peter@FreeBSD.org
text:
Like I said in email *multiple* times before, I needed somebody to patch the www/moinmoin port to include the email verify patch and test that it actually works.

I even provided pointers to the patch that I wanted integrated into the freebsd port.

Since it was finally obvious that nobody was going to do any of the basic help I  asked for, I took time out of moving house and did the work myself as local patches in the cluster package tree.

So now, account creation is enabled, with a recaptcha and email verification links.  Self created users still need to email wiki-admins@ and be added to an ACL (via the gui) so they can edit anything at all, but there is no requirement to have the accounts folks do anything anymore.

I have already provided a copy of the one configuration file for the wiki in email.  As it now contains recaptcha keys, I won't be providing it again in public.

Aside from manual account creation and disabling of accounts, there are no other user serviceable parts of the wiki.  Everything else is administered via the GUI, including adding people to an ACL.

This is the wrong location for a bug like this. If you want clusteradm attention, use admbugs.
